title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: Given API requirements, configuring labretriever use different set endpoints simply means overwriting database_info. details API expectations may found documentation

what: Append an additional endpoint to a given URL — apply_additional_endpoints
title: Append an additional endpoint to a given URL — apply_additional_endpoints
text: function appends additional endpoint given URL ensuring trailing slashes base URL.

title: Append an additional endpoint to a given URL — apply_additional_endpoints
Append an additional endpoint to a given URL — apply_additional_endpoints
text: url character string representing base URL additional endpoint appended. additional_endpoint character string representing additional endpoint appended base URL.

title: Append an additional endpoint to a given URL — apply_additional_endpoints
text: character string representing new URL additional endpoint appended.

what: Apply filters to a URL — apply_filters_to_url
title: Apply filters to a URL — apply_filters_to_url
text: function appends filter strings given URL based provided filter_list. first checks filter_list valid, appends filters URL.

title: Apply filters to a URL — apply_filters_to_url
Apply filters to a URL — apply_filters_to_url
text: url base URL filters applied. filter_list named list filters apply URL. token user authentication token.

title: Apply filters to a URL — apply_filters_to_url
text: URL filters appended filter_list valid, otherwise returns original URL.

title: Apply filters to a URL — apply_filters_to_url
code: if (FALSE) url <- "https://api.example.com/data" filter_list <- list(id = 1, category = "sample") token <- "your_auth_token" apply_filters_to_url(url, filter_list, token) #> Error in as.character(x): cannot coerce type 'closure' to vector of type 'character'  # Example usage of apply_additional_endpoints function base_url <- "https://api.example.com/resource/" endpoint <- "count" new_url <- apply_additional_endpoints(base_url, endpoint) print(new_url) # Output: "https://api.example.com/resource/count" #> [1] "https://api.example.com/resource/count"

what: Create a URL for the given tablename with optional filtering — create_url
title: Create a URL for the given tablename with optional filtering — create_url
text: function creates URL specified tablename combining base URL endpoint tablename. provided, also appends filter strings URL. filter list provided, user authentication token required.

title: Create a URL for the given tablename with optional filtering — create_url
code: create_url(   tablename,   base_url = database_info$base_url,   filter_list = NULL,   token = NULL )

title: Create a URL for the given tablename with optional filtering — create_url
text: tablename name table create URL. base_url base URL API (default: database_info$base_url). filter_list list filter strings append URL (default: NULL). token user authentication token required adding filter strings (default: NULL).

title: Create a URL for the given tablename with optional filtering — create_url
text: complete URL specified tablename, filter strings appended provided.

title: Create a URL for the given tablename with optional filtering — create_url
code: if (FALSE) { # Without filters create_url("ccexperiment")  # With filters create_url("ccexperiment",             filter_list = list("id=1", "category=sample"),             token = "your_auth_token") }

what: Database Information for the Yeast Compendium API — database_info
title: Database Information for the Yeast Compendium API — database_info
text: list containing base URL various API endpoints interact Yeast Compendium API.

title: Database Information for the Yeast Compendium API — database_info
Database Information for the Yeast Compendium API — database_info
text: list containing following elements: base_url base URL API (character string). endpoints list API endpoints (character strings) various resources: auth_token Authentication token endpoint. chrmap Chromosome map endpoint. genes Genes endpoint. genes_with_effects Genes effects endpoint. promoterregions Promoter regions endpoint. promoterregions_targets Promoter regions targets endpoint. harbisonchip Harbison ChIP endpoint. harbisonchip_with_annote Harbison ChIP annotations endpoint. kemmerentfko Kemmeren TF knockout endpoint. mcisaaczev McIsaac ZEV endpoint. background Background endpoint. cctf CC TF endpoint. cctf_tf_list dataframe columns tf_id, tf_locus_tag, tf_gene describes TFs interrogated calling cards ccexperiment CC experiment endpoint. hops HOPS endpoint. hopsreplicatesig HOPS replicate significance endpoint. hopsreplicatesig_with_annote HOPS replicate significance annotations endpoint. qcmetrics QC metrics endpoint. qcmanualreview QC manual review endpoint. qcreview endpoint summarized QC view users want interactive qcr1tor2 QC R1 R2 endpoint. qcr2tor1 QC R2 R1 endpoint. qctftotransposon QC TF transposon endpoint. expression Expression endpoint. pagination_info Pagination information endpoint. row_count Row count endpoint.

title: Database Information for the Yeast Compendium API — database_info
code: # Accessing the base_url from the database_info object database_info$base_url #> [1] "http://127.0.0.1:8000" # Accessing the genes endpoint from the database_info object database_info$endpoints$genes #> [1] "/api/v1/genes/"

Retrieve the fields of a given table from a fields endpoint — get_field_info
text: url base URL endpoint, "http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/genes/". token API authentication token, required. fields_endpoint endpoint retrieving fields, default set "fields/".

title: Retrieve the fields of a given table from a fields endpoint — get_field_info
text: list containing readable fields, writable fields, automatically generated fields.

title: Retrieve the fields of a given table from a fields endpoint — get_field_info
code: if (FALSE) { # Get the fields of a given table from a fields endpoint url <- "https://example.com/api/genes/" token <- "my_token" table_fields <- get_field_info(url, token) }

what: Get pagination information from a REST_FRAMEWORK endpoint — get_pagination_info
title: Get pagination information from a REST_FRAMEWORK endpoint — get_pagination_info
text: function retrieves pagination information given REST_FRAMEWORK endpoint. returns list default page size page size limit.

title: Get pagination information from a REST_FRAMEWORK endpoint — get_pagination_info
Get pagination information from a REST_FRAMEWORK endpoint — get_pagination_info
text: url character string specifying view send request. constructed using database_info -- see example. token authentication token use accessing endpoint pagination_endpoint default, set database_info$endpoints$pagination_info appended appropriately url. example, interested retrieving pagination settings following table: http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/genes/, pass url, without trailing /, pagination_endpoint, provided database_info. URL queried something along lines : http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1/genes/pagination_info/. course, endpoint must exist work.

title: Get pagination information from a REST_FRAMEWORK endpoint — get_pagination_info
text: list default page size page size limit

title: Get pagination information from a REST_FRAMEWORK endpoint — get_pagination_info
code: if (FALSE) { token <- get_user_auth_token(   paste0(     database_info$base_url,     database_info$endpoints$auth_token   ),   "username",   "password" )  url <- paste0(database_info$base_url, database_info$endpoints$chrmap) # Retrieve the pagination information pagination_info <- get_pagination_info(url, token)  print(pagination_info) }

title: Retrieve the total number of records from a count endpoint — get_record_count
code: if (FALSE) { # Get the total number of records from a count endpoint count_url <- "https://example.com/api/count" token <- "my_token" total_records <- get_record_count(count_url, token) }

Get User Authentication Token — get_user_auth_token
text: function retrieves authentication token specified URL using username password.

title: Get User Authentication Token — get_user_auth_token
Get User Authentication Token — get_user_auth_token
text: url character string specifying view send request. constructed using database_info -- see example. username character string specifying user's username. password character string specifying user's password.

title: Get User Authentication Token — get_user_auth_token
text: authentication token character string.

title: Get User Authentication Token — get_user_auth_token
text: save auth token public repository. example, might put .Renviron make sure .Renviron .gitignore. Otherwise, save outside github tracked directory otherwise ensure pushed github

title: Get User Authentication Token — get_user_auth_token
code: if (FALSE) { # Note: This example is wrapped in \dontrun{} because it requires a valid API # endpoint and authentication credentials. Please replace the example URL, # username, and password with your actual API endpoint and credentials to test # the function.  # Get the user authentication token auth_url <- "https://your-api.com/api/auth-token/" username <- "your_username" password <- "your_password" token <- get_user_auth_token(auth_url, username, password) print(token)  # Set the token as an environment variable # usethis::edit_r_environ("project") # This opens a file called .Renviron in the project directory  # IMPORTANT: Immediately create a .gitignore and add # .Renviron to it to avoid committing the login token to Github  # Add your token to the .Renviron file like this # TOKEN=<your token>  # Reload the project, and now you can access the environmental # variable token # print(Sys.getenv("TOKEN"))  # Define the count endpoint URL record_count_url <- "https://your-api.com/data_table/count/"  # Use the authentication token with httr like this: # token <- Sys.getenv("TOKEN") my_api_call <- labretreiver::retrieve(record_count_url, token)  # Get the content of the API call httr::content(my_api_call) }
